acceptanceCriteria: releaseCiFact returns an `unverifiable` (fail-closed) result when the required-check set is empty, instead of vacuously reporting success, and a unit test covers this without contacting GitHub.

docs/release.md clarifies that on a merged main commit only the quality check runs for the push, so the pre-tag gate uses --required-check quality and requiring the PR-only rigor check on the main SHA would fail closed unpassably.
All original GH-8 acceptance criteria still hold; npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict all pass with the rebuilt dist/rigor.cjs.
The full change set stays within the eight allowed paths and re-verifies with no scope violations.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
